dependencies.description:
Boost provides free peer-reviewed portable C++ source libraries,
emphasizing libraries that work well with the C++ Standard Library.
Boost libraries are intended to be widely useful, and usable across a
broad spectrum of applications. The Boost license encourages both
commercial and non-commercial use.
dependencies.name: boost

dependencies.description:
A cross-platform, open-source build system. CMake is a family of tools
designed to build, test and package software.
dependencies.name: cmake

dependencies.description:
CMake Modules developed in the context of various FAIR
(https://www.gsi.de/en/researchaccelerators/fair) projects.
dependencies.name: faircmakemodules

dependencies.description: Lightweight and fast C++ Logging Library
dependencies.name: fairlogger

dependencies.description:
C++ Message Queuing Library and Framework
(https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.1689985)
dependencies.name: fairmq

dependencies.description: Bundle package providing default environment for FAIR software
dependencies.name: fairsoft-bundle

dependencies.description: Memory Efficient Serialization Library
dependencies.name: flatbuffers

dependencies.description:
fmt (formerly cppformat) is an open-source formatting library. It can be
used as a safe alternative to printf or as a fast alternative to C++
IOStreams.
dependencies.name: fmt

dependencies.description:
Simulation software using Monte Carlo methods to describe how particles
pass through matter.
dependencies.name: geant3

dependencies.description:
Geant4 is a toolkit for the simulation of the passage of particles
through matter. Its areas of application include high energy, nuclear
and accelerator physics, as well as studies in medical and space
science.
dependencies.name: geant4

dependencies.description: Geant4 VMC implements the Virtual Monte Carlo (VMC) for Geant4.
dependencies.name: geant4-vmc

dependencies.description:
GNU Make is a tool which controls the generation of executables and
other non-source files of a program from the program's source files.
dependencies.name: gmake

dependencies.description: Google test framework for C++. Also called gtest.
dependencies.name: googletest

dependencies.description: A small, fast binary interchange format convertible to/from JSON
dependencies.name: msgpack-c

dependencies.description:
Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed. It differs from
other build systems in two major respects: it is designed to have its
input files generated by a higher-level build system, and it is designed
to run builds as fast as possible.
dependencies.name: ninja

dependencies.description: Google's data interchange format.
dependencies.name: protobuf

dependencies.description:
PYTHIA is a program for the generation of high-energy physics events,
i.e. for the description of collisions at high energies between
elementary particles such as e+, e-, p and pbar in various combinations.
PYTHIA6 is a Fortran package which is no longer maintained: new
prospective users should use Pythia8 instead. This recipe includes
patches required to interoperate with Root.
dependencies.name: pythia6

dependencies.description:
The Pythia program is a standard tool for the generation of events in
high-energy collisions, comprising a coherent set of physics models for
the evolution from a few-body hard process to a complex multiparticle
final state.
dependencies.name: pythia8

dependencies.description: ROOT is a data analysis framework.
dependencies.name: root

dependencies.description:
Virtual Geometry Model (VGM) is a geometry conversion tool, actually
providing conversion between Geant4 and ROOT TGeo geometry models.
dependencies.name: vgm

dependencies.description: The Virtual Monte Carlo (VMC) library
dependencies.name: vmc

dependencies.description: A YAML parser and emitter in C++
dependencies.name: yaml-cpp

description:
C++ simulation, reconstruction and analysis framework for particle
physics experiments
name: fairroot

variants.description: Build systems supported by the package
variants.name: build_system

variants.description: Use the specified C++ standard when building.
variants.name: cxxstd

variants.description: the build system generator to use
variants.name: generator

variants.description: Enable simulation engines and event generators
variants.name: sim
